q: How many lines of symmetry does a regular pentagon have?
sol: A regular polygon has as many lines of symmetry as it has sides.

q: Which of the following letters has horizontal symmetry?
sol: The letter 'E' can be divided into two equal halves by a horizontal line.

q: A scalene triangle has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: A scalene triangle has no equal sides or angles, so it has no line of symmetry.

q: An isosceles triangle has how many lines of symmetry?
sol: An isosceles triangle has one line of symmetry passing through the vertex between the equal sides.

q: Which figure has infinite lines of symmetry?
sol: Any line passing through the center of a circle is a line of symmetry.

q: The letter 'H' has:
sol: 'H' can be cut down the middle vertically and across the middle horizontally.

q: A rectangle has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: A rectangle has two lines of symmetry joining the midpoints of opposite sides.

q: Which of these regular polygons has 6 lines of symmetry?
sol: A regular hexagon has 6 equal sides and 6 lines of symmetry.

q: The image of an object in a mirror is called its:
sol: Mirror symmetry is essentially the reflection of the object.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a rhombus have?
sol: A rhombus has two lines of symmetry, which are its diagonals.

q: Which letter looks exactly the same after a vertical reflection?
sol: 'A' is vertically symmetrical; its left side is a mirror image of its right side.

q: A square has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: A square has 4 lines: two joining midpoints of opposite sides and two diagonals.

q: Which of the following shows no symmetry?
sol: Letter 'Z' has rotational symmetry but no line (reflectional) symmetry.

q: The line of symmetry of a line segment is its:
sol: The perpendicular bisector divides a segment into two reflected halves.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a semi-circle have?
sol: A semi-circle has only one line of symmetry, which is the perpendicular bisector of its diameter.

q: An equilateral triangle has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: It has three lines of symmetry, each passing through a vertex and the midpoint of the opposite side.

q: The number of lines of symmetry in a regular octagon is:
sol: A regular octagon has 8 sides and 8 lines of symmetry.

q: Which of these has both horizontal and vertical symmetry?
sol: The letter 'O' (and circle) is symmetrical about any diameter.

q: A parallelogram generally has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: A standard parallelogram has no lines of symmetry unless it is a rectangle or rhombus.

q: Symmetry is often found in:
sol: Symmetry is a universal concept found in flowers, buildings, and paintings.

q: Which letter has ONLY vertical symmetry?
sol: 'U' has a vertical line of symmetry; 'C', 'D', and 'E' have horizontal ones.

q: How many lines of symmetry does an isosceles trapezium have?
sol: It has one line of symmetry connecting the midpoints of the parallel sides.

q: A figure is said to have symmetry if it can be folded so that the two parts:
sol: Symmetry implies a perfect correspondence in size and shape on opposite sides of a line.

q: Which of these digits has two lines of symmetry?
sol: The digit '8' can be split vertically and horizontally into matching halves.

q: A kite has how many lines of symmetry?
sol: A kite has one line of symmetry along its main diagonal.

q: Which letter remains unchanged after a 180-degree rotation but has no line symmetry?
sol: 'N' has rotational symmetry of order 2 but no line of symmetry.

q: If an object is reflected in a mirror, the distance of the image from the mirror is ___ the distance of the object.
sol: The mirror line is the perpendicular bisector of the segment joining object and image.

q: How many lines of symmetry does the word 'WOW' have (if written vertically)?
sol: If aligned vertically, 'W-O-W' can be split by a single vertical line.

q: The letter 'X' has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: 'X' has a vertical and a horizontal line of symmetry.

q: Which of the following triangles has exactly 3 lines of symmetry?
sol: Equilateral triangles are the only triangles with 3 lines of symmetry.

q: A regular polygon with 'n' sides has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: This is a fundamental rule for regular polygons.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a point have?
sol: Every line passing through a point can be considered a line of symmetry in a sense, but usually, we refer to figures.

q: The letter 'S' has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: Letter 'S' has rotational symmetry but no reflectional symmetry.

q: Which shape has exactly two lines of symmetry?
sol: A rectangle has two (vertical and horizontal midlines).

q: Symmetry along a line is called:
sol: Line symmetry or reflectional symmetry is folding over a line.

q: How many lines of symmetry are in a regular hexagon?
sol: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry.

q: Which letter has horizontal symmetry only?
sol: 'K' has a horizontal line of symmetry, while W, Y, and A have vertical ones.

q: A circle is symmetrical about:
sol: Any line passing through the center (diameter) divides it into two semi-circles.

q: The digit '0' has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: The digit '0' (if oval) has two lines of symmetry.

q: Which regular polygon has the least number of lines of symmetry?
sol: The equilateral triangle is the regular polygon with the fewest sides (3).

q: How many lines of symmetry does a star with 5 points have?
sol: A 5-pointed star (regular) has 5 lines of symmetry.

q: A paper folded once and cut at the fold will show ___ symmetry.
sol: The fold line acts as the axis of symmetry.

q: Which of these letters has NO symmetry?
sol: Letter 'F' cannot be divided into matching halves.

q: A line of symmetry is also known as a ___ line.
sol: It is called a mirror line because one side is the reflection of the other.

q: How many lines of symmetry does an angle have?
sol: An angle has one line of symmetry, which is its angle bisector.

q: A regular heptagon has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: Regular polygon with 7 sides has 7 lines of symmetry.

q: The letter 'M' has vertical symmetry. Its reflection in a horizontal mirror would look like:
sol: Reflecting 'M' horizontally flips it upside down to look like 'W'.

q: Which of the following shapes has 4 lines of symmetry?
sol: Square has 4 lines (2 diagonals + 2 midlines).

q: How many lines of symmetry does a quadrant (quarter circle) have?
sol: A quadrant has one line of symmetry (the 45-degree bisector).

q: The image of a clock showing 3:00 in a vertical mirror will show:
sol: The hands flip across the vertical axis, moving from 3 to 9.

q: Which of the following is an example of bilateral symmetry?
sol: The human face has a single vertical line of symmetry.

q: A scalene triangle has ___ lines of symmetry.
sol: Zero.

q: What tool is useful to determine the line of symmetry?
sol: Placing a mirror on the line helps check if the reflection matches the other side.

q: Which letter has both horizontal and vertical lines of symmetry?
sol: Letter 'I' has both.

q: How many lines of symmetry are in a non-isosceles trapezium?
sol: Zero.

q: The alphabet 'B' has ___ symmetry.
sol: Horizontal.

q: The number of lines of symmetry in a regular decagon is:
Which figure has only 1 line of symmetry?
sol: Isosceles triangle.

q: A line segment of length 10cm has its line of symmetry at:
sol: At the midpoint (5cm).

q: Which of these flowers often shows radial symmetry?
sol: Sunflowers have multiple lines of symmetry through the center.

q: An equilateral triangle can be folded ___ ways to match halves.
sol: 3 ways.

q: The line of symmetry of a circle is its:
sol: Diameter.

q: Which letter has no lines of symmetry but has rotational symmetry?
sol: Z.

q: Symmetry in nature is most evident in a:
sol: Butterfly wings are perfectly symmetrical.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a regular nonagon have?
Which of the following is NOT symmetrical?
sol: Parallelogram (unless it's a special case like a rectangle).

q: The mirror image of 'L' will face:
sol: It will face the opposite horizontal direction.

q: If we reflect the letter 'D' vertically, it looks like:
sol: 'D' has horizontal symmetry, but reflecting it vertically (mirror on the right) makes it look like a backwards D.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a heart shape have?
sol: 1 (vertical).

q: A figure with point symmetry has rotational symmetry of order:
sol: Point symmetry means 180-degree rotation (Order 2).

q: Which of these is a vertically symmetric capital letter?
sol: T.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a cross (+) with equal arms have?
sol: 4 (2 midlines + 2 diagonals).

q: Which shape has rotational symmetry but no line symmetry?
sol: A pinwheel rotates to look the same but doesn't have a fold line.

q: A regular polygon's lines of symmetry intersect at its:
sol: Center.

q: How many lines of symmetry are in the letter 'Q'?
sol: 0 (because of the tail).

q: The reflection of a right-hand in a mirror looks like a:
sol: Lateral inversion.

q: Which of these letters has NO horizontal symmetry?
sol: T has only vertical symmetry.

q: A line of symmetry of an equilateral triangle is also its:
sol: In equilateral triangles, these coincide.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a regular dodecagon have?
The letter 'W' has:
sol: Vertical.

q: A figure with reflection symmetry is also called:
sol: Symmetric.

q: Which shape has the most lines of symmetry?
sol: Circle (infinite).

q: How many lines of symmetry does the number '3' have?
sol: 1 (horizontal).

q: Symmetry based on turning a figure around a fixed point is:
sol: Rotational symmetry.

q: Which letter has rotational symmetry of order 2?
sol: S.

q: If you rotate a square by 90 degrees, it looks:
sol: Order of rotation is 4.

q: A regular triangle is also known as:
sol: Equilateral.

q: The word 'AMBULANCE' is written backwards on the front of the vehicle because of:
sol: To appear correct in rearview mirrors.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle's diagonal represent?
sol: The diagonal of a rectangle is NOT a line of symmetry.

q: Which digit has exactly one line of symmetry?
sol: 3 (horizontal).

q: A figure that can be divided into two matching parts by a line has:
sol: Line symmetry.

q: The letter 'Y' has ___ line(s) of symmetry.
sol: 1 (vertical).

q: How many lines of symmetry does an arrow (head + shaft) have?
sol: 1 (along the shaft).

q: Which of these is NOT vertically symmetric?
sol: P.

q: An equilateral triangle has rotational symmetry of order:
The order of rotational symmetry for a circle is:
sol: Infinite.

q: Which regular polygon has 4 lines of symmetry?
sol: Square.

q: Symmetry helps in making things look:
sol: Balanced.

q: The letter 'O' has rotational symmetry of order:
sol: Infinite.

q: How many lines of symmetry does a kite have?
